note: February at Aihole: 16-30°C, dry Deccan light on 120+ Chalukya sandstone temples. Early mornings ideal for the open temple complex; shadeless by noon.
February at Alampur: 18-31°C, winter sun rakes the nine Navabrahma Shiva temples where the Tungabhadra meets Krishna. Open dawn to dusk, quietest mid-week.
February at Alleppey: 23-32°C, dry backwaters at their calmest — coconut-palm canals, houseboats, and paddy fields sharp against clear skies. Book houseboats ahead.
February at Auroville: 21-31°C, dry and cool enough for cycling the red-earth paths. Matrimandir gardens and organic farms run peak visitor hours.
February at Bekal: 23-32°C, the Arabian Sea calm against the laterite fort walls. Afternoon light hits the bastions best; sunset from the seaward rampart is the moment.
February at Beypore: 23-32°C, dry Malabar coast — ideal for watching the Uru dhow shipyard at work. Sandbar walks at low tide; the port is active year-round.
February at Bidar: 15-30°C, crisp Deccan plateau mornings perfect for the fort's 37-bastion circuit. Bidriware workshops open through the day in the old city.
February in Chennai: 22-31°C, the Marina Beach season — dry, breezy, and Margazhi music-festival energy still lingering in the sabhas of Mylapore.
February at Cherai Beach: 23-32°C, dolphins pass offshore and Chinese fishing nets dip through still mornings. Long shallow shoreline; low-risk swim conditions.
February in Chettinad: 20-32°C, dry enough to wander the mansion villages by mid-morning. Courtyards cool, spicy Chettinad meals best before the afternoon heat climbs.
February at Chidambaram: 22-31°C, dry Cauvery-delta light on the Nataraja Temple. Abhishekam darshans before 8am are the clearest window.
February at Chitradurga: 16-30°C, cool mornings make the seven-walled fort climb feasible before sun hits the granite. Carry water; shade is thin on the upper ramparts.
February at Daman: 17-30°C, dry Arabian coast with the Portuguese forts catching slanting winter light. Sea is calm; beaches wide at low tide.
February at Dhanushkodi: 23-31°C, dry and steady at India's tip where Bay of Bengal meets Indian Ocean. Jeeps from Rameswaram run the sand track to the 1964 ruins.
February at Diu: 18-29°C, dry Portuguese island — Diu Fort, Nagoa Beach, and the sea breeze make long walks easy. Roads quiet mid-week.
February at Gulbarga: 15-30°C, cool dry mornings for the Bahmani fort and the fully-roofed Jama Masjid. Sufi dargahs active through the day.
February at Guruvayur: 23-33°C, dry Krishna-temple weather — pre-dawn darshan queues move fastest. Temple elephants visible at the nearby Punnathurkotta sanctuary.
February at Kanchipuram: 22-32°C, dry silk-city season — 1,000 temples navigable by auto between shrines. Silk weavers work through the morning.
February at Kannur: 23-32°C, Theyyam season in full flow in village shrines across North Malabar. Drive-in Muzhappilangad beach hard-packed for vehicles.
February at Kanyakumari: 23-30°C, dry three-seas confluence — clearest horizons for the simultaneous sunrise and sunset view the tip is known for.
February at Karaikal: 23-31°C, dry Cauvery-delta enclave — French fort ruins, coastal temples, and a quiet seafront before summer heat returns.
February at Karwar: 21-32°C, calm Arabian Sea along the Konkan — Devbagh Island boat runs operate smoothly and Tagore's beach sits empty mid-week.
February at Kashid: 19-32°C, white-sand Konkan beach at its cleanest — weekend Mumbai crowds thicken but weekdays stay calm. Two-hour drive from Alibaug.
February in Fort Kochi: 23-32°C, dry heritage-quarter weather — Chinese fishing nets, Jew Town spice lanes, and the Biennale venues still walkable on foot.
February at Kollam: 23-32°C, dry Ashtamudi backwaters — the 8-hour canoe cruise to Alleppey runs daily and Munroe Island canals stay clear of weed.
February at Konark: 17-30°C, dry Odisha coast — every stone wheel and sculpture on the Sun Temple readable in sharp low-angle winter light.
February at Kovalam: 24-31°C, dry Arabian Sea swells moderate enough for lighthouse-beach swimming. Ayurveda season runs peak; stays book out on weekends.
February at Kozhikode: 23-32°C, dry Malabar port weather for walking Mananchira, SM Street, and the beach. Biryani lunches best before the afternoon humidity rises.
February at Kumarakom: 23-32°C, Vembanad Lake at its birding peak — Siberian migrants still wintering, houseboats cruise calm water dawn to dusk.
February at Kumbakonam: 22-31°C, dry temple-city weather for walking between the 188 shrines and the UNESCO Airavatesvara at nearby Darasuram. Degree-coffee stalls run all morning.
February at Kumbalangi: 23-32°C, dry backwater village weather — crab farms and Chinese nets easy to watch from the canoe paths. Homestays fill on weekends.
February at Kuttanad: 23-32°C, dry below-sea-level paddy country — the underwater-cropping landscape visible from the bunds between canals. Houseboats connect to Alleppey.
February at Madurai: 22-32°C, dry Meenakshi Temple season — 33,000 sculptures readable in morning light and the nightly midnight closing ritual draws steady crowds.
February at Mahabalipuram: 22-31°C, dry Coromandel coast — Pallava rock-cuts, Shore Temple, and the beach all in one day. Sculpture school chisels active through the morning.
February at Malvan: 20-31°C, calm Konkan sea — Sindhudurg Fort boat access runs reliably and scuba visibility at Tarkarli stays good through the month.
February at Mandrem: 21-32°C, dry North Goa beach — yoga and kitesurfing season in full swing. Quieter stretch than Morjim or Ashvem; long walkable shoreline.
February at Mandvi: 15-29°C, cool Kutch coast — the 400-year-old Vijay Vilas palace and the dhow-building yards easy to visit before the afternoon sun. Beach stays calm.
February at Marari: 23-32°C, dry fishing-village beach — long clean sand, no watersports scene, catamarans pulled up by the morning catch. Weekends book out.
February at Morjim: 21-32°C, olive ridley nesting still running on Goa's quiet turtle beach. Estuary birding and calm sea; peak-season rates through the month.
February at Nalsarovar: 14-30°C, the wetland at full migratory peak — flamingos, pelicans, and cranes in the thousands. Boat rides launch at dawn for the best light.
February in Pondicherry: 22-31°C, dry French Quarter weather — White Town cycling, Promenade Beach walks, and cafe life all work. Peak tourist month on weekends.
February at Poovar: 23-32°C, dry estuary weather where river, sea, and beach meet. Boat access to the island resorts runs smoothly; Neyyar birding nearby.
February at Rajahmundry: 20-32°C, Godavari at her calmest — boat rides into the Papikondalu gorge run full-day and the river city's ghats stay active at dusk.
February at Rameswaram: 23-31°C, dry island-pilgrimage weather — the Char Dham temple corridor walkable comfortably and Pamban Bridge crossings clear of sea spray.
February at Reis Magos: 21-32°C, dry Mandovi estuary — the restored Portuguese fort catches morning sun across from Panaji. Art shows run through peak Goa season.
February at Sasan Gir: 14-31°C, cool dry mornings with lion sightings at their most reliable. Forest dept safari slots book out; permits ideally arranged in advance.
February at Srirangam: 22-32°C, dry Cauvery-island temple weather — the 156-acre Ranganathaswamy complex and its 21 gopurams walkable before noon heat.
February at Thanjavur: 21-32°C, dry Chola-heartland light — the UNESCO Brihadeeswara vimana sharpest from mid-morning. Art-gallery hours align with cooler afternoons.
February at Tharangambadi: 22-31°C, dry Danish-colonial coast — Fort Dansborg, the old Protestant church, and a quiet seafront walkable end to end.
February at Trichy: 22-32°C, dry enough for the 83m Rock Fort climb by mid-morning. Srirangam temple nearby makes for a full day pairing.
February in Trivandrum: 23-32°C, dry capital weather — Padmanabhaswamy temple queues steady, museum-palace precinct walkable, zoo and botanical gardens at peak visitor month.
February at Udupi: 22-32°C, Krishna temple and Malpe Beach both in their dry-season stride. Dosa-belt restaurants busy through lunch; St Mary's Island boats run all day.
February at Varkala: 23-32°C, cliff-beach peak season — Papanasam sunrise dips, yoga shalas full, and the clifftop promenade lively till late. Stays book out on weekends.
May at Anamalai: 18-30°C, pre-monsoon Western Ghats — lion-tailed macaques active in the canopy and Topslip safari plus bamboo rafting slots run through the morning.
May at Coonoor: 14-26°C, Nilgiri tea country at its flowering peak — quieter than Ooty, and the toy-train stretch through the estates runs clear pre-monsoon skies.
May at Kodaikanal: 12-24°C, pre-monsoon hill-station season — star-lake boat rides, Coaker's Walk mornings, and pine-forest trails all at their coolest before June rains.
May at Kotagiri: 14-26°C, the oldest Nilgiri hill station at peak quiet — tea-estate walks, Catherine Falls viewpoint, and Toda tribal villages cool through the day.
May at Mudumalai: 20-32°C, pre-monsoon Nilgiri biosphere — elephant herds gather at the dwindling waterholes and jeep safaris run both morning and afternoon slots.
May at Ooty: 12-25°C, peak Nilgiri summer-holiday season — tea estates green, toy train running full, and botanical garden flower show opens mid-month. Stays book out early.
May at Thrissur: 25-34°C, Thrissur Pooram month — Kerala's biggest temple festival brings a full elephant line-up at Vadakkunnathan, panchavadyam drums, and all-night fireworks.
May at Yercaud: 15-28°C, Eastern Ghats coffee country at its flowering peak — the lake, orange groves, and Pagoda Point viewpoint all walkable in cool day weather.
October at Ambaji: 20-33°C, post-monsoon Aravalli foothills — the Shakti Peeth hilltop temple draws Navaratri pilgrims and the approach road stays clear of rain.
October at Coonoor: 13-24°C, Nilgiri post-monsoon — tea slopes washed green, toy-train views clearest of the year, and Sim's Park at peak bloom. Crowds lighter than Ooty.
October at Halebidu: 18-30°C, post-monsoon Hoysala country — the 240 wall panels on Hoysaleshwara sharpest in softer post-rain light. Pair with Belur for a full day.
October at Hogenakkal: 22-32°C, post-monsoon Cauvery at full volume — coracle rides through the falls run all day and the riverside oil-massage tradition is at its busiest.
October at Idukki: 18-28°C, post-monsoon cardamom hills — the arch dam reservoir at full level, Idukki wildlife sanctuary reopens, and spice-plantation walks dry out.
October at Kodaikanal: 12-22°C, post-monsoon hill clarity — star-lake reflections crisp, Coaker's Walk views unobstructed, and pine-forest trails firm underfoot after the rains.
October at Kotagiri: 13-25°C, post-monsoon Nilgiris — Catherine Falls at peak volume, tea slopes freshly green, and Kodanad viewpoint clear of cloud cover on most mornings.
October at Munnar: 14-24°C, tea estates washed emerald post-monsoon — Eravikulam reopens, Anamudi views clear, and the Kurinji hills start the walk into their bloom year.
October at Nelliyampathy: 15-26°C, post-monsoon Palakkad hills — tea and coffee estates lush, birding hides active, and the ghat road drier than the June-September stretch.
October at Ooty: 10-22°C, post-monsoon Queen of the Nilgiris — tea estates green, toy train runs the full route, and Doddabetta clear most mornings. Shoulder season; stays easier.
October at Ponmudi: 16-26°C, post-monsoon Western Ghats — butterfly numbers peak, the 22 hairpin bends from Trivandrum dry out, and the Gold Peak viewpoint clears by mid-morning.
October at Thekkady: 15-27°C, Periyar reopens post-monsoon — lake boat safaris run both shifts, elephant herds visible from the water, and spice-plantation trails firm underfoot.
October at Vagamon: 14-25°C, post-monsoon pine meadows at their greenest — paragliding season resumes, rolling grassland trails dry out, and sunset from Thangal Para stays clear.
October at Wayanad: 17-28°C, post-monsoon Western Ghats — Edakkal caves reopen, Pookode Lake fills, waterfalls at Meenmutty and Soochipara run full, and trails dry through the month.
October at Yercaud: 15-27°C, post-monsoon Eastern Ghats — coffee beans ripening on the estates, the lake at full level, and orange groves heading into harvest. Shoulder-season stays easier.
